requirements: The core contributions of the paper 'Prefill-as-a-Service: KVCache of Next-Generation Models Could Go Cross-Datacenter' have been reproduced.

sub_tasks.requirements: The infrastructure setup for the PrfaaS-PD case study has been established.
Code has been implemented to deploy a PrfaaS cluster consisting of 32 H200 GPUs (or comparable high-compute-throughput accelerators) dedicated to long-context prefill.

verification.spec: Inspect the deployment configuration (e.g., cluster config file, launch script, or YAML) and verify that a PrfaaS cluster is defined with 32 H200 GPUs allocated for long-context prefill. Check that the cluster role is labeled as 'prfaas' or equivalent and that the GPU count and type match. Use importlib to load the config module and assert num_gpus==32 and gpu_type=='H200'.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to deploy a local PD cluster of 64 H20 GPUs (or comparable bandwidth-optimized accelerators) with 800 Gbps RDMA interconnect per node, operating in PD-disaggregated mode.

verification.spec: Inspect the deployment configuration for the local PD cluster and verify it consists of 64 H20 GPUs with 800 Gbps RDMA interconnect per node, configured in PD-disaggregated mode. Load the cluster config via importlib and assert num_gpus==64, gpu_type=='H20', interconnect_bandwidth_gbps==800, and mode=='PD-disaggregated'.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to deploy a homogeneous PD baseline cluster of 96 H20 GPUs.

verification.spec: Inspect the baseline cluster configuration and verify it defines a homogeneous PD cluster of 96 H20 GPUs. Load the config via importlib and assert num_gpus==96, gpu_type=='H20', cluster_type=='homogeneous_PD'.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to connect the PrfaaS and local PD clusters via a VPC network providing approximately 100 Gbps aggregate cross-cluster bandwidth.

verification.spec: Inspect the network configuration files / topology descriptor and verify a VPC peering (or equivalent) link is declared between the PrfaaS cluster and the local PD cluster with an aggregate bandwidth of approximately 100 Gbps.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented for intra-cluster RDMA-based KVCache transfer between PD nodes within each cluster.

verification.spec: Import the implementation module and verify a class/function exists for intra-cluster RDMA-based KVCache transfer between prefill and decode nodes (e.g. a transport backend class with send/recv methods that selects an RDMA backend when both endpoints are in the same cluster). Instantiate it in a mocked two-node setup and verify a small tensor is transferred end-to-end.

sub_tasks.requirements: The internal 1T-parameter hybrid model following the Kimi Linear architecture has been set up.
The model is deployed at 8 GPUs per instance for both prefill and decode.

verification.spec: Inspect the vLLM launch configuration (e.g., tensor_parallel_size + pipeline_parallel_size, or world_size per instance) for both prefill and decode roles and verify GPUs-per-instance equals 8.

sub_tasks.requirements: The model is served using in-house vLLM (or vLLM v0.5.9+) supporting hybrid attention architectures.

verification.spec: Run `python -c 'import vllm; print(vllm.__version__)'` and assert version is >= 0.5.9, or verify the in-house vLLM fork is installed and supports hybrid KDA+MLA attention by importing the hybrid attention manager class.

sub_tasks.requirements: The PrfaaS-PD architecture and its three subsystems (compute, network, storage) have been implemented.
The hybrid prefix cache pool described in Section 3.2 has been implemented.

Code has been implemented so linear attention recurrent states are managed at request-level granularity (cached state size is independent of input length and reusable only when cached length matches exactly).

verification.spec: Construct two requests with identical prefixes of length L and L' (L ≠ L'). Process them via the hybrid cache manager and assert: (1) the linear-attention recurrent state allocated for each request has the same fixed size regardless of L vs L'; (2) request B with prefix length L' ≠ L cannot reuse request A's linear state (cache miss), but a request with prefix length exactly equal to L can reuse it (cache hit).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented so full-attention KVCache is managed at block-level granularity, supporting partial prefix matching and growing linearly with input length.

verification.spec: Submit a request of length L tokens to the hybrid cache manager. Verify that the full-attention KVCache for that request occupies ceil(L/block_size) blocks (linear growth). Then submit a second request sharing a prefix of length P < L (P a block-aligned multiple); verify the cache manager reports a prefix hit of P tokens and only allocates new blocks for the non-shared suffix.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented so that linear states and full-attention KVCache are managed by separate KVCache groups with aligned block sizes, sharing a unified block pool, based on the vLLM hybrid KVCache manager.

verification.spec: Inspect the hybrid KVCache manager implementation and verify: (a) two distinct KVCacheGroup objects exist (one for linear-attention recurrent states, one for full-attention KVCache); (b) both groups share a single underlying block pool (same block allocator instance); (c) block sizes are aligned across the groups. Import the class and assert via attribute introspection.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to categorize cache blocks as prefix-cache blocks (must be fully populated before reuse, intra-cluster only, block-aligned).

verification.spec: Instantiate the hybrid prefix cache pool and allocate a sequence of block-aligned blocks for a request. Verify that blocks marked as prefix-cache become reusable across requests only after being fully populated (test that a partially populated prefix block is NOT returned by lookup for a second request matching the same prefix), and that block size alignment is enforced.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to categorize cache blocks as transfer-cache blocks (hold KVCache produced at the tail of a prefill request for cross-cluster transfer, discarded after transfer completes, can be any length).

verification.spec: Allocate transfer-cache blocks at the tail of a prefill request with non-block-aligned length. Verify that (a) the allocation succeeds for arbitrary lengths, (b) the blocks are routable across clusters, and (c) after a simulated cross-cluster transfer completion the pool reclaims the blocks (they are not retained for prefix reuse).

sub_tasks.requirements: The global KVCache manager has been implemented to maintain KVCache metadata across all clusters and compute prefix-match information for routing.
Code has been implemented so that the global KVCache manager tracks the storage locations of all cached entries across PrfaaS and PD clusters.

verification.spec: Populate the global KVCache manager with cached entries on cluster A and cluster B for distinct prefixes. Query the manager for prefix matches of a new request and verify it returns per-cluster matched lengths (l_prfaas and l_pd) consistent with the populated entries.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented so that the manager computes prefix-match information per cluster for routing decisions and ensures only the incremental portion of cached requests is transferred across clusters.

verification.spec: Submit a request whose prefix is partially cached in cluster X. Verify (via instrumented byte counters) that only the incremental (uncached) portion of KVCache is transferred across clusters, not the cached prefix portion. Compare bytes transferred against expected S_kv(l_total - l_cached).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented for cache rebalancing to mitigate hotspots when sufficient inter-cluster bandwidth is available.

verification.spec: Configure a scenario where one cluster has a hot prefix entry receiving heavy traffic while another cluster has spare capacity, and set inter-cluster bandwidth as abundant. Run the cache rebalancer and verify it migrates/replicates the hot entry to the under-loaded cluster, reducing the hot cluster's request rate for that prefix.

sub_tasks.requirements: Stable, high-throughput Ethernet transport mechanisms for cross-cluster KVCache transfer have been implemented.
Code has been implemented for layer-wise prefill pipelining that overlaps KVCache generation with cross-cluster transmission.

verification.spec: Instrument the PrfaaS prefill engine to record timestamps of (a) layer-i KVCache produced and (b) layer-i KVCache transmit-start over the network. For a multi-layer prefill, assert that transmit-start of layer i occurs before completion of layer N (where N is the last layer), demonstrating overlap between computation and transmission rather than strict end-of-prefill batching.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented for multi-connection TCP transport to fully utilize the available inter-cluster Ethernet bandwidth.

verification.spec: Configure the cross-cluster transport with a single Ethernet link and N>1 parallel TCP connections. Send a large KVCache payload and verify aggregate goodput is significantly higher than a single-connection baseline (e.g. >=80% of link capacity vs <50% with one connection) on a loopback or emulated link.

sub_tasks.requirements: The PrfaaS-PD throughput model from Section 3.4.1 has been implemented.
Code has been implemented to compute PD-P (prefill-in-PD) throughput as Θ_pd-p = N_p / (P · T_prefill(l_short)), determined solely by compute capacity.

verification.spec: Call the throughput model with sample inputs (N_p, P, T_prefill(l_short)) and verify the returned Theta_pd-p equals N_p / (P * T_prefill(l_short)) to within floating-point tolerance. Test multiple input combinations including edge cases (N_p=1, large l_short).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to compute PD-D (decode) throughput as Θ_pd-d = N_d · BS_max / (P · T_decode · L_out), governed by SLO constants.

verification.spec: Call the decode-throughput function with sample (N_d, BS_max, P, T_decode, L_out) and verify the returned Theta_pd-d equals N_d * BS_max / (P * T_decode * L_out) for several parameter combinations.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to compute end-to-end system throughput as Λ_max = min(Θ_prfaas / p, Θ_pd-p / (1-p), Θ_pd-d), respecting the routing split p.

verification.spec: Call the end-to-end throughput function with synthetic stage rates Θ_prfaas=2.0, Θ_pd-p=1.0, Θ_pd-d=5.0 req/s and p=0.5. Expected Λ_max = min(2/0.5, 1/0.5, 5) = min(4, 2, 5) = 2 req/s. Assert returned value equals 2 within 1e-6. Repeat with p=0.8 and assert correct min.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to compute cluster egress bandwidth requirement B_out = N_prfaas/P · S_kv(L_avg) / T_prefill(L_avg) following Equation (2).

verification.spec: Call the egress-bandwidth computation function with (N_prfaas, P, S_kv, T_prefill, L_avg) and verify it returns N_prfaas/P * S_kv(L_avg) / T_prefill(L_avg) within floating-point tolerance. Test that B_out scales linearly with N_prfaas.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to compute per-instance KV throughput Φ_kv = S_kv(l) / T_prefill(l) following Equation (1).

verification.spec: Call the Phi_kv function with profiled S_kv and T_prefill values from Table 5 (e.g. l=32K: S_kv=701.3 MiB, T_prefill=1.84 s) and verify the returned value matches 3.19 Gbps (within 5%). Test for multiple sequence lengths.

sub_tasks.requirements: The throughput-optimal configuration solver from Section 3.4.2 has been implemented.
Code has been implemented so that the optimal threshold t balances PrfaaS and PD-P throughput at the intersection Θ_prfaas / p = Θ_pd-p / (1-p) (Equation 7).

verification.spec: Fix N_p=3, N_d=5 and sweep t over the supported range. Verify the optimizer's selected t* is the value where Theta_prfaas(t)/p(t) intersects Theta_pd-p(t)/(1-p(t)) within numerical tolerance, and that no other t in the grid yields higher min(Theta_prfaas/p, Theta_pd-p/(1-p)).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented so the optimal N_p/N_d ratio satisfies Θ_prfaas + Θ_pd-p = Θ_pd-d (Equation 8).

verification.spec: Fix t at optimum and sweep N_p/N_d. Verify the optimizer's chosen (N_p*, N_d*) satisfies |Theta_prfaas + Theta_pd-p - Theta_pd-d| <= |Theta_prfaas + Theta_pd-p - Theta_pd-d| at all other adjacent (N_p, N_d) grid points (i.e. balancer condition holds at the optimum).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented so that for a request length distribution, p = P(L > t), l_long = E[L | L > t], and l_short = E[L | L ≤ t] are computed correctly.

verification.spec: Provide a truncated log-normal distribution (mu=9.90, sigma=1.00, [128,128K]) and t=19.4K. Verify the implementation returns p=P(L>t) ~= 0.496, l_long = E[L|L>t] ~= 44K, and l_short = E[L|L<=t], each within 2% of values computed by an independent scipy.stats.truncnorm-like reference.

sub_tasks.requirements: The dual-timescale scheduling algorithm from Section 3.4.3 has been implemented.
Short-term bandwidth- and cache-aware routing has been implemented.
Code has been implemented for length-based threshold routing: requests with incremental prefill length l > t are routed to a PrfaaS cluster, while requests with l ≤ t are handled by a PD-P node.

verification.spec: Invoke the router with a synthetic threshold t=1000 on a sequence of requests with incremental lengths {500, 1500, 999, 2000, 1001}. Assert that requests with l ≤ 1000 (500, 999) are routed to PD-P and requests with l > 1000 (1500, 2000, 1001) are routed to a PrfaaS cluster.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to continuously monitor PrfaaS egress utilization and request queue depth and trigger short-term routing adjustments when utilization or queuing surges.

verification.spec: Simulate egress utilization rising past a configured threshold (e.g. 80%) and queue depth rising. Verify the scheduler's monitoring loop triggers a routing-policy update (callback invoked, or new t value emitted) within one monitoring interval.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented so that on initialization or policy update, the scheduler profiles current compute capacity and egress bandwidth, then re-searches for the optimal threshold t based on the incremental prefill-length distribution after prefix matching.

verification.spec: Trigger a policy update event with a modified profile (e.g. reduced egress bandwidth) and an updated incremental-length distribution (after prefix matching). Verify the scheduler re-profiles inputs and the recomputed threshold t* differs from the previous value in the expected direction (higher t when bandwidth shrinks).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented for the bandwidth-scarce routing rule: evaluate prefix caches per cluster independently — if l_total - l_pd ≤ t route to PD-P, otherwise route to PrfaaS.

verification.spec: Configure the scheduler with bandwidth-scarce mode (e.g., set utilization above the congestion threshold). For a request with l_total=10000, l_pd=8000, l_prfaas=9500, and t=1500: assert routing is PD-P because l_total - l_pd = 2000 > t? Recompute: with t=2500, l_total - l_pd = 2000 ≤ t → PD-P. Then with l_total=10000, l_pd=5000, t=2500: l_total - l_pd = 5000 > t → PrfaaS. Verify the router ignores l_prfaas in scarce mode.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented for the bandwidth-abundant routing rule: use l_prefix = max(l_prfaas, l_pd) — if l_total - l_prefix ≤ t prefill in PD-P, otherwise in PrfaaS, performing cross-cluster cache transfer if the longer-cache cluster differs from the compute cluster.

verification.spec: Configure scheduler in bandwidth-abundant mode. Submit a request with l_total=10000, l_pd=3000, l_prfaas=8000, t=2500. Assert: l_prefix = max(3000,8000) = 8000; l_total - l_prefix = 2000 ≤ t → prefill in PD-P; AND because the longer cache (PrfaaS) differs from the compute cluster (PD), a cross-cluster cache transfer of 8000 tokens is triggered. Also test the opposite case (l_total - l_prefix > t) and verify routing to PrfaaS.

sub_tasks.requirements: Long-term traffic-driven allocation re-optimization has been implemented.
Code has been implemented to monitor queue depth and utilization at each stage to identify whether prefill or decode is the binding constraint.

verification.spec: Inject test workloads where (a) PD-D queue depth grows while PD-P/PrfaaS are idle, and (b) the reverse. Verify the scheduler's diagnostic API correctly reports the binding stage ('decode-bound' or 'prefill-bound') in each case.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to periodically re-evaluate load balance and convert nodes between prefill and decode roles within the PD cluster, adjusting N_p and N_d to restore optimality conditions.

verification.spec: Start with N_p=5, N_d=3 and inject sustained decode-bound traffic. Verify that after one long-term reallocation interval the scheduler converts at least one node from prefill to decode role (N_p decreases, N_d increases) and that t is re-optimized accordingly. Symmetric test for prefill-bound traffic.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented so that when instance counts change, the routing threshold t is re-optimized accordingly.

verification.spec: Invoke the scheduler's long-term re-optimization routine with two different (N_p, N_d) configurations (e.g., N_p=3,N_d=5 and N_p=5,N_d=3) while holding hardware/bandwidth/distribution constant. Verify that the returned routing threshold t differs between the two calls, and that for each call t satisfies Equation 7 (PrfaaS-bound throughput equals PD-P-bound throughput within 5%) using the model's profiling curves.

sub_tasks.requirements: The workload and request generation for the case study have been implemented.
Code has been implemented to generate request input lengths following a truncated log-normal distribution with μ = 9.90, σ = 1.00, truncated to [128, 128K].

verification.spec: Call the workload generator to produce 100k input lengths. Assert: (1) all samples are within [128, 128*1024]; (2) sample mean of log(length) is within 0.05 of 9.90; (3) sample std of log(length) is within 0.05 of 1.00; (4) no values lie outside the truncation bounds.

sub_tasks.requirements: The mean input length of the generated workload is approximately 27K tokens.

verification.spec: Generate the workload using a truncated log-normal distribution (mu=9.90, sigma=1.00, truncated to [128, 128K]) as specified in Section 4.1, sample at least 100K requests, and compute the empirical mean input length. Assert the mean is approximately 27K tokens.

sub_tasks.requirements: Output length is fixed at 1024 tokens for all requests.

verification.spec: Inspect the workload-generation configuration (e.g., YAML / Python config) and confirm that the output_length field is fixed to 1024 tokens. Additionally, sample 100 generated request specs from the harness and assert all have max_output_tokens == 1024.

sub_tasks.requirements: Decode SLO (excluding speculative decoding) is set to 40 tokens/s.

verification.spec: Inspect the SLO configuration object/file used by the scheduler/throughput model and confirm the decode SLO is set to 40 tokens/s (i.e., T_decode = 1/40 s = 25 ms per token).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented for arrivals to follow a uniform aggregate arrival rate Λ as in the throughput model.

verification.spec: Run the request-generator with a target arrival rate Λ for 60s and collect inter-arrival timestamps. Verify the empirical mean rate is within 5% of Λ and that the inter-arrival CV is consistent with Poisson/uniform arrivals (CV ≈ 1 for Poisson or near 0 for deterministic) as documented; in either case requests must arrive at constant aggregate rate not in bursts.

sub_tasks.requirements: Profiling of the 1T hybrid model has been performed and used as inputs to the throughput model.
Code has been implemented to benchmark prefill latency T_prefill(l) at input lengths 1K, 8K, 32K, and 128K on 8×H200 with in-house vLLM.

verification.spec: Run the prefill-latency benchmark script with the internal 1T hybrid model on an 8xH200 instance using the in-house vLLM build. Confirm the script outputs T_prefill values for input lengths {1K, 8K, 32K, 128K} and writes them to a results file (e.g., CSV/JSON) consumable by the throughput model.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to measure KVCache size S_kv(l) per request at input lengths 1K, 8K, 32K, and 128K.

verification.spec: Run a KVCache-size measurement script that for input lengths {1K, 8K, 32K, 128K} runs prefill on the 1T hybrid model and records the total bytes of KVCache (linear-attention recurrent state + MLA full-attention KV blocks) produced per request. Verify outputs are written for all four lengths.

sub_tasks.requirements: Measured S_kv ≈ 190.8 MiB at 1K input length (paper_value=190.8, paper_unit=MiB, verification.type=measured, paper_category=claim).

verification.spec: Run prefill on the 1T hybrid model with a 1K-token request on 8xH200 and measure the total KVCache size (sum of all layers' KV/state bytes). Report S_kv in MiB and compare against the paper value.

sub_tasks.requirements: Measured S_kv ≈ 308.9 MiB at 8K input length.

verification.spec: Run prefill on the 1T hybrid model with an 8K-token request on 8xH200 and measure total KVCache size in MiB. Compare against paper value.

sub_tasks.requirements: Measured S_kv ≈ 701.3 MiB at 32K input length.

verification.spec: Run prefill on the 1T hybrid model with a 32K-token request on 8xH200 and measure total KVCache size in MiB. Compare against paper value.

sub_tasks.requirements: Measured S_kv ≈ 2316.3 MiB at 128K input length.

verification.spec: Run prefill on the 1T hybrid model with a 128K-token request on 8xH200 and measure total KVCache size in MiB. Compare against paper value.

sub_tasks.requirements: Baseline deployments for comparison have been implemented.

The naive heterogeneous PD baseline (4 PrfaaS H200 instances and 8 H20 decode instances, no length-based routing or scheduling, all prefill on H200 and all decode on H20) has been set up and evaluated.

verification.spec: Run the naive heterogeneous PD baseline configuration: instantiate the throughput model with 4 H200 prefill instances and 8 H20 decode instances, with no length-based routing (all prefill on H200, all decode on H20). Verify the output reports Λ_max≈2.45 req/s, Θ_prfaas≈2.45, Θ_pd-d≈6.25, and that no routing threshold t is applied.

sub_tasks.requirements: The case study results from Section 4 have been reproduced.
The optimal PrfaaS-PD configuration parameters from Table 6 are reproduced.
The optimal routing threshold t is approximately 19.4K tokens.

verification.spec: Run the throughput model grid search (Section 3.4) using the profiling data in Table 5 with Np=3, Nd=5, and find the routing threshold t that maximizes Lambda_max as the intersection of Theta_prfaas/p and Theta_pd-p/(1-p). Assert the optimal t is approximately 19.4K tokens.

sub_tasks.requirements: The optimal allocation under PrfaaS-PD is N_prfaas=4, N_p=3, N_d=5 instances.

verification.spec: Run the PrfaaS-PD grid-search optimizer with the case-study parameters (32 H200 PrfaaS GPUs at 8 GPUs/instance => 4 PrfaaS instances; 64 H20 GPUs at 8 GPUs/instance => 8 PD instances total; 100 Gbps cross-cluster bandwidth; log-normal length distribution μ=9.90,σ=1.00; output=1024). Assert the optimal allocation returned is N_prfaas=4, N_p=3, N_d=5.

sub_tasks.requirements: Under the optimal threshold, approximately 49.6% (≈50%) of all requests are routed to the PrfaaS cluster.

verification.spec: Using the truncated log-normal request length distribution (mu=9.90, sigma=1.00, truncated to [128, 128K]) and the throughput model from Section 3.4 with profiling data from Table 5, solve the grid search for optimal threshold t. Then compute the fraction of requests with L > t under the input length distribution. Verify this fraction is approximately 0.496 (i.e., ~50% of requests routed to PrfaaS).

sub_tasks.requirements: The mean uncached length of PrfaaS-offloaded requests E[L | L > t] is approximately 44K tokens.

verification.spec: Using the truncated log-normal request length distribution (mu=9.90, sigma=1.00, truncated to [128, 128K]) and the optimal threshold t=19.4K, compute E[L | L > t] by numerical integration or Monte Carlo sampling. Assert the result is approximately 44K tokens.

sub_tasks.requirements: The optimal homogeneous PD baseline allocation is N_p=9, N_d=3 instances.

verification.spec: Run the homogeneous PD baseline grid search over (N_p, N_d) with N_p+N_d=12 H20 instances. Assert the optimal allocation returned is N_p=9, N_d=3 instances.

sub_tasks.requirements: The reproduced throughput numbers match the claims of Table 6.
PrfaaS-PD achieves approximately 1.54× the throughput of the homogeneous PD baseline (54% higher).

verification.spec: Compute the ratio Λ_max(PrfaaS-PD) / Λ_max(homogeneous PD) using the throughput model outputs for both configurations. Assert ratio ≈ 1.54.

sub_tasks.requirements: PrfaaS-PD achieves approximately 32% higher throughput than the naive heterogeneous PD baseline.

verification.spec: Apply the throughput model from Section 3.4 to both the PrfaaS-PD configuration (32 H200 + 64 H20, optimal t=19.4K, Np=3, Nd=5) and the naive heterogeneous PD configuration (4 PrfaaS instances + 8 decode instances on H20, no length-based routing) using Table 5 profiling data. Compute Lambda_max for each and verify the ratio (PrfaaS-PD / naive) - 1 is approximately 0.32 (i.e., 3.24/2.45 ≈ 1.32).

sub_tasks.requirements: Naive heterogeneous PD achieves approximately 1.16× throughput over the homogeneous baseline.

verification.spec: Compute the throughput ratio Λ_max(naive heterogeneous PD) / Λ_max(homogeneous PD) = 2.45 / 2.11 using the throughput-model outputs. Compare against paper value.

sub_tasks.requirements: At equal cost, PrfaaS-PD provides approximately 15% throughput gain over the homogeneous baseline.

verification.spec: Compute equal-cost throughput gain: scale the PrfaaS-PD deployment cost to match the homogeneous PD baseline cost using documented H200/H20 unit costs, recompute Λ_max under the cost-matched configuration, and divide by homogeneous PD throughput minus 1. Assert the gain is approximately 0.15 (15%).

sub_tasks.requirements: TTFT measurements match the claims in Table 6.
PrfaaS-PD achieves mean TTFT ≈ 2.22 s and P90 TTFT ≈ 3.51 s.

verification.spec: Run the PrfaaS-PD throughput model with the case-study configuration (32 H200 PrfaaS + 64 H20 PD, t=19.4K, N_p=3, N_d=5, log-normal length distribution mu=9.90 sigma=1.00 truncated [128,128K], output=1024 tokens, SLO 40 tok/s) and simulate request scheduling at lambda_max. Compute mean and P90 TTFT across simulated requests and compare to 2.22 s and 3.51 s.

sub_tasks.requirements: Homogeneous PD baseline has mean TTFT ≈ 4.44 s and P90 TTFT ≈ 9.73 s.

verification.spec: Run the throughput model for the homogeneous PD baseline (96 H20 GPUs, N_p=9, N_d=3) with the same workload distribution as the case study. Simulate scheduling at lambda_max and compute mean and P90 TTFT, comparing to 4.44 s and 9.73 s.

sub_tasks.requirements: Naive heterogeneous PD has mean TTFT ≈ 1.74 s and P90 TTFT ≈ 3.51 s.

verification.spec: Run the throughput-model TTFT computation for the naive heterogeneous PD baseline (4 PrfaaS H200, 8 H20 decode, no scheduling) under the workload distribution. Report mean TTFT (expect 1.74 s) and P90 TTFT (expect 3.51 s). Verify the reported P90 TTFT.

sub_tasks.requirements: PrfaaS-PD reduces mean TTFT by approximately 50% and P90 TTFT by approximately 64% compared to the homogeneous PD baseline.

verification.spec: Compute (1 - PrfaaS-PD_meanTTFT / homogeneousPD_meanTTFT) and (1 - PrfaaS-PD_P90TTFT / homogeneousPD_P90TTFT) from the two simulated configurations and verify they are approximately 0.50 and 0.64 respectively.

sub_tasks.requirements: Cross-datacenter bandwidth utilization measurements match the paper's claims.
The measured aggregate PrfaaS cluster egress load is approximately 13 Gbps under the deployed workload.

verification.spec: With t=19.4K and the truncated log-normal length distribution, compute p=P(L>t) (~0.496) and l_long=E[L|L>t] (~44K). Then compute B_out = (N_prfaas/P) * S_kv(l_long) / T_prefill(l_long) using the Table 5 profile for the internal 1T model with N_prfaas=4 instances. Verify result is ~13 Gbps.

sub_tasks.requirements: The PrfaaS egress consumes approximately 13% of the 100 Gbps cross-cluster Ethernet link.

verification.spec: Under PrfaaS-PD optimal configuration, compute aggregate PrfaaS egress = (Λ_max * p) * S_kv(l_long) with l_long=44K tokens, then divide by 100 Gbps. Verify the egress is ~13 Gbps (~13% of the link).

sub_tasks.requirements: The grid search visualization (Figure 5) is reproduced.
Code has been implemented to plot Λ_max vs. (N_p, N_d) allocation at fixed t, showing peak at N_p=3, N_d=5 with Λ_max ≈ 3.24 req/s (Figure 5a).

verification.spec: Run the Figure 5a plotting script that fixes t=19.4K and sweeps (N_p, N_d) with N_p+N_d=8 in the PrfaaS-PD setup. Verify (1) a plot file is produced; (2) the curve crossing point/maximum is at N_p=3, N_d=5; (3) the peak Λ_max value is within 0.1 of 3.24 req/s.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to plot Λ_max vs. threshold t at fixed (N_p=3, N_d=5), showing intersection of PrfaaS-bound and PD-Prefill-bound curves at t=19.4K with Λ_max≈3.24 req/s (Figure 5b).

verification.spec: Import sglang; assert sglang.__version__.startswith('0.5.9'). Launch a minimal SGLang runtime with a tiny model (e.g. facebook/opt-125m), submit one generation request, and assert it returns a non-empty string.
verification.paper_cite: Table 3 caption: 'benchmarked on 8×H200 with SGLang v0.5.9'

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that a vLLM build (the in-house variant referenced by the paper, approximated by upstream vLLM) is installed and supports loading a hybrid-attention model architecture (Kimi Linear-style KDA + MLA interleaved layers). A smoke test confirms vLLM can be imported and instantiate an LLM engine with a small substitute model.

verification.spec: Import vllm; assert vllm.__version__ is defined. Instantiate vllm.LLM with a small open model and assert the engine initializes. Additionally check that vllm supports a hybrid KV cache manager (attribute or module presence: vllm.v1.core.kv_cache_manager or equivalent hybrid manager hook).
verification.paper_cite: Table 5 caption: 'Prefill latency is benchmarked on 8×H200 with in-house vLLM'

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that an 8×H200 GPU instance is detected and usable for prefill latency / KV throughput benchmarking, matching the hardware configuration used for Tables 3 and 5.

verification.spec: Run `nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name,memory.total --format=csv`; assert exactly 8 GPUs are reported and all are H200 (name contains 'H200') with memory >= 140GB each. Additionally verify torch.cuda.device_count() == 8 and torch.cuda.get_device_properties(0).name contains 'H200'.
verification.paper_cite: Table 3 caption and Section 4.1 hardware setup

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that a cross-cluster network link with aggregate bandwidth of approximately 100 Gbps (within 20% tolerance) is provisioned between the PrfaaS cluster and the local PD cluster, and measured end-to-end via a TCP/iperf-style throughput test.

verification.spec: Run a multi-connection TCP throughput test (e.g. iperf3 with -P 8) between a node in the PrfaaS cluster and a node in the local PD cluster for at least 30 seconds. Parse the achieved aggregate bandwidth in Gbps. Assert |measured - 100| / 100 <= 0.20.
verification.paper_cite: Section 4.1: 'aggregate cross-cluster bandwidth of approximately 100 Gbps'

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the intra-cluster RDMA fabric on the local PD cluster delivers at least 800 Gbps per node (within 20% tolerance), verified via an RDMA bandwidth microbenchmark (e.g. ib_write_bw or perftest), confirming the network configuration described in Section 4.1 for PD KVCache transfers.

verification.spec: Run ib_write_bw (or equivalent perftest) between two PD-cluster nodes summing across all RDMA NICs on the host for at least 30 seconds. Parse aggregate per-node bandwidth in Gbps. Assert |measured - 800| / 800 <= 0.20.
verification.paper_cite: Section 4.1: 'PD cluster ... with 800 Gbps RDMA interconnect per node'

sub_tasks.requirements: Method components (from critique splits and additions)

Code implements the PrfaaS cluster throughput formula Theta_prfaas = min(N_prfaas / T_prefill(l_long), B_out / S_kv(l_long)) as described by the model in Section 3.4.1 (equation depicted at picture 284x25).

verification.spec: Import the throughput model module; call compute_prfaas_throughput(N_prfaas=4, T_prefill_long=7.40, S_kv_long=2316.3*8/1024, B_out=100e9) with known values and verify the result equals min(N_prfaas/T_prefill_long, B_out/S_kv_bytes) within 1e-6 relative tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code implements the PD-P throughput formula Theta_pd-p = N_p / T_prefill(l_short) as defined in Section 3.4.1 (equation at picture 243x24).

verification.spec: Call compute_pdp_throughput(N_p=3, T_prefill_short=0.72) and assert result == 3/0.72 within 1e-6.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code implements the PD-D (decode) throughput formula Theta_pd-d = N_d * BS_max / (L_out * T_decode) as defined in Section 3.4.1 (equation at picture 244x23).

verification.spec: Call compute_pdd_throughput(N_d=5, BS_max=256, L_out=1024, T_decode=1/40) with known values and verify result equals N_d*BS_max/(L_out*T_decode) within 1e-6 relative tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code implements the converging-pipeline constraint p*Lambda <= Theta_prfaas, (1-p)*Lambda <= Theta_pd-p, Lambda <= Theta_pd-d as a feasibility check (Section 3.4.1, picture 234x61).

verification.spec: Call is_feasible(Lambda, p, Theta_prfaas, Theta_pdp, Theta_pdd) with feasible and infeasible inputs; assert returns True for feasible (e.g., p=0.5,Lambda=3.0 with Theta_prfaas=2.0,Theta_pdp=2.0,Theta_pdd=4.0) and False when any constraint violated.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code implements the threshold-optimality condition Theta_prfaas/p = Theta_pd-p/(1-p) used by grid search to choose t (Section 3.4.2, picture 233x24).

verification.spec: Construct a synthetic profile where the optimum is known analytically; run the threshold search and assert the returned t* satisfies |Theta_prfaas/p - Theta_pdp/(1-p)| / Theta_pdd < 0.05.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code implements the prefill/decode-allocation optimality condition Theta_prfaas + Theta_pd-p = Theta_pd-d, used to choose N_p/N_d in the local PD cluster (Section 3.4.2, picture 247x11).

verification.spec: Run the grid search over (N_p, N_d) with N_p+N_d=8 using the case-study profile (Table 5); assert the returned optimum is (N_p=3, N_d=5) as stated in Table 6.

sub_tasks.requirements: Ablation studies from the paper

Code has been implemented and run such that an ablation comparing bandwidth-scarce vs bandwidth-abundant cache-aware routing rules demonstrates the two distinct routing behaviors described in Section 3.4.3

verification.spec: Implement both routing rules: (1) bandwidth-scarce uses per-cluster prefix l_pd, l_prfaas independently; (2) bandwidth-abundant uses l_prefix=max(l_pd, l_prfaas) and triggers cross-cluster transfer when better cache is elsewhere. Construct test cases where l_pd=10K, l_prfaas=30K, l_total=40K, t=19.4K: under bandwidth-scarce rule the request routes to PrfaaS (since 40K-10K=30K>t); under bandwidth-abundant rule the request routes to PD-P with cross-cluster cache transfer (since 40K-30K=10K<=t). Assert both behaviors match.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that an ablation disabling length-based threshold routing (sending all prefill to PrfaaS) yields lower system throughput than the threshold-routed PrfaaS-PD configuration

verification.spec: Run throughput model with t=19.4K (selective offload) and with t=0 (all prefill offloaded to PrfaaS); compute Λmax for both; assert Λmax(t=19.4K) / Λmax(t=0) >= 1.25 (since naive heterogeneous achieves only 1.16x vs 1.54x for PrfaaS-PD, a ~32% gap)

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that disabling cross-cluster cache transfer/rebalancing under bandwidth-abundant conditions results in measurably more redundant prefill computation than enabling it

verification.spec: Simulate a workload of 1000 requests with prefix-cache hits unevenly distributed (e.g., 70% have longer prefix in PD, 30% have longer prefix in PrfaaS). Compute total uncached prefill tokens under two policies: (a) with cross-cluster cache transfer enabled using l_prefix=max(l_pd, l_prfaas); (b) disabled, using only local cluster's prefix. Assert total uncached tokens for (a) is at least 10% lower than (b).
